pullRequest.title: Fix Django signals firing when viewing historical versions in admin
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes #936: Resolves issue where Django model signals were incorrectly fired when users viewed historical versions of objects in the admin interface.

## Problem

Django model signals (`pre_save`, `post_save`, `pre_delete`, `post_delete`, `m2m_changed`) were being fired when users simply viewed historical versions in the admin, causing unintended side effects from signal handlers (e.g., message queue publishing, search index updates) that should only occur during actual data changes.

## Solution

- **Added thread-safe `mute_signals` context manager** in `reversion/utils.py`
- **Modified `VersionAdmin._reversion_revisionform_view`** to conditionally mute signals
- **Mute signals only for GET requests** (viewing), allow signals for POST (reverting)
- **Added comprehensive test coverage** for all 5 Django model signals
- **Ensured proper thread safety** with signal.lock and cache management

## Test Plan

- [x] Added `AdminRevisionViewSignalsTest` with comprehensive signal testing
- [x] Tests both GET (signals muted) and POST (signals fire) scenarios  
- [x] All existing admin tests pass (16/16)
- [x] New signal tests pass (2/2)
- [x] Code passes flake8 linting

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

pullRequest.title: Implement date-based ordering and custom version sort
pullRequest.body:
This PR adds two new sort options for the Version queryset:

- `history_order_by_date` - modeled after `history_latest_first`, when set to `True` this causes the Versions to be sorted by `revision__date_created` instead of `pk`. This can be used in combination with `history_latest_first` to achieve a reverse chronological sort. Default is `False` for backwards compatibility.
- `get_version_ordering()` method - allows for a fully custom and/or dynamic sort implementation in client admins.

Tests and docs are also updated accordingly.

Resolves #991
